title: Iranian state media say country's supreme leader Ali Khamenei is dead
subhead: Iran's state media confirmed then news early Sunday. Hours earlier, President Donald Trump announced that Khamenei had been killed in a joint American-Israeli operation.

title: Iran's state media say 100 killed in strike on girls' school
subhead: Footage broadcast by state media shows the rubble of a girls' school in Minab where 108 people were reportedly killed, according to Iranian press reports. Tehran has accused the United States and Israel of an 'atrocious crime.'

title: Trump gives in to the temptation of regime change in Iran with Operation Epic Fury
subhead: The American president, in coordination with Israel, has embarked on a war with an unpredictable duration. This runs counter to his promises to the MAGA movement, which has opposed any prolonged military engagement abroad.

title: Iran attacks luxury hotels and airports in Dubai
subhead: Explosions rocked many of the city's major landmarks and revenue-earners, including the Palm Jumeirah man-made island, the Burj Al Arab ultra-luxury hotel and Dubai airport.

title: Israel rallies in national unity to support attack on Iran
subhead: Ahead of October parliamentary elections, on Saturday, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's opponents voiced their support for the US-Israel joint operation against Iran's regime. Israeli public opinion was also ready for a new offensive.

title: Five Paris restaurants serving mouthwatering ramen
subhead: A staple of Japanese cuisine, this noodle soup takes on subtle flavors depending on whether it is made with pork, fish or vegetables. Here are our picks for the best places to enjoy excellent ramen in the French capital.

title: 'The rebuilding of Iran's ballistic capabilities is more concerning than a rapid nuclear restart'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, researcher Nicole Grajewski analyzes the current state of Tehran's military capabilities eight months after the Israeli-American strikes in June 2025.
